finding_name: DNS Server Cache Snooping Remote Information Disclosure
fallback_commands:
sh -c 'out=$(dig @{host} -p {port} google.com A +norecurse +time=5 +tries=1 2>&1); printf "%s
" "$out"; printf "%s
" "$out" | grep -qi "status: noerror" && printf "%s
" "$out" | grep -Eqi "ANSWER: [1-9]" && echo CACHE_SNOOPING_ENABLED || echo CACHE_SNOOPING_DISABLED'
not_validated_if: 0 domains were found to be cached
analyst_note: DNS cache snooping via timed mode. Cached domains indicate the server is resolving and caching external queries — confirming recursive resolution is exposed.
